mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand recovery, reducing recession probability and supporting higher equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint upward trends in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPIAUCSL readings exceeding 197.0 indicate accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 197.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending and corporate profitability to drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5%, or the interaction effect shows negative predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which translates into sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals sudden manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during recovery phases.
